pir: Will the tracked competitor make a material pricing change within 30 days, and if so in which direction and on which product line?

ground_truth.state: Cirrus has approved a list-price increase on its Enterprise tier, framed internally as margin recovery, effective within the window. This period also happens to be unusually newsworthy for Cirrus for unrelated reasons: a new executive hire, a new data-center region, a conference keynote, an open-source contribution, an industry award, and a charity initiative all generate well-documented, high-grade coverage that has nothing to do with pricing. The pricing decision itself is reflected in only a few items: an internal finance contact, a sales-compensation change favoring higher contract value, and a leaked order form with revised list rates. The correct read is that the volume of unrelated, high-grade coverage should not move the judgment or inflate confidence; only the three diagnostic items should.

signal_stream.content: Cirrus announced the hire of a new Chief Marketing Officer, formerly of a well-known SaaS company.
Cirrus opened a new data-center region in Singapore to support APAC customers.
The Cirrus CTO delivered a well-received keynote on platform reliability at a major industry conference.
Cirrus open-sourced an internal logging library, which was well received by developers.
Cirrus won an industry award for customer support quality.
Cirrus announced a charity matching program for employee donations.
Cirrus published its annual sustainability report.
A reliable contact in Cirrus finance reports that leadership approved a list-price increase on the Enterprise tier, framed internally as margin recovery, effective within the month.
An updated Cirrus sales-compensation plan, obtained through a recruiting channel, raises quota credit for higher-contract-value deals on Enterprise.
A leaked Cirrus enterprise order form shows list rates revised upward versus the prior version.
